inclusion_criteria: Participants are undergraduate dental students (e.g., BDS/DDS) in preclinical or clinical courses.
Intervention uses team-based learning (TBL) as the primary instructional approach.

Empirical comparative design (e.g., randomized controlled trial, quasi-experimental, controlled pre–post) comparing TBL to another pedagogy (e.g., lecture, problem-based learning, enquiry-based learning, case-based learning).
Reports quantitative outcomes for student satisfaction and/or academic performance (e.g., exam scores, OSCE, grades, validated satisfaction scales) with data suitable for extraction.
exclusion_criteria: Population is not undergraduate dental students, or mixed cohorts where undergraduate dental data cannot be isolated (e.g., residents, postgraduates, other health professions).
TBL is not the primary intervention, lacks a comparator pedagogy, or compares only variations of TBL without another teaching method.
Non-empirical or non-comparative studies (e.g., reviews, protocols, editorials, purely qualitative reports, cross-sectional surveys without an instructional intervention).
Does not report quantitative measures of student satisfaction or academic performance, or provides insufficient data for outcome extraction.
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria to include empirical comparative studies evaluating team-based learning versus other teaching pedagogies among undergraduate dental students, reporting quantitative outcomes on student satisfaction and/or academic performance.

included_articles.title: Team-based learning versus traditional lectures in local anesthesia course: A cross-sectional study exploring dental students' perception and performance.
Comparison between flipped classroom and team-based learning in fixed prosthodontic education.
Effects of team-based learning on fixed prosthodontic education in a Japanese School of Dentistry.
